panels.description: Medium shot, slightly low angle: The Protagonist (I), a man in late 19th-century attire, stands alone in a cavernous, dark industrial setting, complex machinery faintly visible in the gloom. He looks thoughtful, a hint of regret on his face. He holds his empty hands up slightly, indicating a lack of resources. A single, small matchbox is clutched in one hand. Soft, dim light from an unseen source barely illuminates the scene, casting long shadows.

bubbles.text: If only I had a Kodak! I could have flashed that glimpse of the Underworld in a second.
But all I have are my hands, feet, and teeth; these, and four safety-matches that still remained to me.
I was afraid to push my way in among all this machinery in the dark, and my matches were dwindling.

panels.description: Medium close-up shot: The Protagonist's face in profile, eyes wide with apprehension in near darkness. A gaunt, skeletal hand with long fingers reaches from the right, delicately touching his cheek and then reaching towards his hand, where a small matchbox is held. Another shadowy, lank hand appears from behind him, plucking at his coat. The background is an indistinct, swirling darkness, emphasizing the unseen threat. A faint, unpleasant aura seems to emanate from the hands.

bubbles.text: Then, as I say, I had four left, and while I stood in the dark, a hand touched mine...
Lank fingers came feeling over my face, and I was sensible of a peculiar unpleasant odour.
I felt the box of matches in my hand being gently disengaged, and other hands behind me plucking at my clothing.

panels.description: Close-up shot, slightly Dutch angle: The Protagonist's face, bathed in heavy, uncertain shadows, eyes wide with a mixture of revulsion and dawning terror. Beads of sweat form on his brow. The unseen touch of the Morlocks is palpable, though they remain unseen, represented by distorted, dark outlines in the background. His expression conveys profound discomfort and the chilling realization of their alien nature.

bubbles.text: The sense of these unseen creatures examining me was indescribably unpleasant.
The sudden realisation of my ignorance of their ways came home to me very vividly in the darkness.

panels.description: Extreme close-up shot, distorted perspective: The Protagonist's mouth wide open in a guttural shout, eyes squeezed shut in pure, unadulterated horror and fear. Veins visibly strained on his neck. The background is a chaotic blur of dark, vaguely humanoid shapes recoiling, then creeping back in again. Dynamic, harsh lighting from an unknown source illuminates his terrified face dramatically.

bubbles.text: I was horribly frightened! I shouted at them as loudly as I could!
They started to move away, and then I could feel them approaching again.

panels.description: Close-up shot, slightly looking down: The Protagonist's face, contorted in a grimace of absolute horror and a violent shiver. His hand is clutched desperately at his throat, eyes wide and bloodshot, fixed on something unseen. Droplets of cold sweat fly from his face. The vague, eerie outlines of the Morlocks are faintly visible in the dark background, accompanied by faint, unsettling, almost mocking 'hee-hee' sound effects emerging from the shadows. Heavy, dark shadows define his face.

bubbles.text: I shivered violently, and shouted again—rather discordantly!
I will confess I was frightfully scared. I clutched at my throat.

panels.description: Medium close-up shot, dramatic low angle: The Protagonist, eyes still wide but now with a spark of desperate resolve, holds up a newly struck, brightly flickering match. The harsh, brief light dramatically illuminates the grotesque, 'chinless, great, lidded, pinkish-grey eyes' of a Morlock, staring blankly and bewilderedly into the flame. Other shadowy Morlock figures with similar features are faintly visible behind it, recoiling slightly from the light. The background is dark, mechanical, and oppressive.

bubbles.text: I struck another match, and waved it in their dazed faces!
You can scarcely imagine how nauseously inhuman they looked in their chinless, great, lidded, pinkish-grey eyes—as they stared in their blindness and bewilderment.
